name: Tooling fragmentation as adoption barrier
description: Participants described the overhead of integrating multiple disparate monitoring tools as a significant barrier to comprehensive LLM quality monitoring, and expressed demand for unified frameworks.
key_evidence: Teams use 3–5 separate tools: Prometheus/Datadog for infra, custom NLI scripts, human review spreadsheets (INT-01, INT-06, INT-12)
Tooling fragmentation creates blind spots at tool boundaries — IQ and SF often monitored by separate teams (INT-05, INT-10)
Demand for unified quality layer explicitly stated (INT-03, INT-09, INT-12, INT-13)
significance_for_qalis: Directly motivates the QALIS unified framework design goal. Validates practitioner demand for integrated assessment.
